session_summary: No-event-time architectural pivot. v0 simplifies dramatically: Beava is Redis with stateful operators, processing-time only — no event-time anywhere, no watermarks, no joins (any kind), no PIT. Five answered design questions locked the shape. Five phase dirs archived (14 streaming-correctness, 14.1 streaming-modifiability, 15 event-time-PIT, 15-snapshot-io-off-main-thread, 14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency). New permanent memory `project_redis_shaped_no_event_time_ever` written; `project_stateful_architecture` Decision 2 (PIT) marked SUPERSEDED. ROADMAP retitled Phase 12, archived 12.5/14/14.1/15, reworked 17, inserted NEW Phase 12.6 (v0 surface reduction — biggest v0 blocker) and NEW Phase 25 (session windows — v0.1+). PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/STATE/CORRECTNESS-PATH all swept to match. Single doc-only commit `2d58d80`. No code changes.
next_focus: v0 polish via Phase 12.6 surface reduction (architectural cleanup) → Phase 13 minimum subset (packaging + quickstart + readme + website final) → ship

completed_this_session.item: Resumed from prior HANDOFF (2026-04-30T00 → consumed and renamed to HANDOFF.consumed-2026-04-30-am.json)
Identified Phase 14 dir collision: `14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency` (pre-pivot stale work) vs `14-streaming-correctness` (canonical roadmap entry); archived stale dir + verified resolver

Reviewed all 4 streaming-correctness plans + surfaced D-08 drift (Phase 13.3 RefCell/borrow_mut narrative dead — apply path is now ServerV18 mio); identified 8 drift items (3 critical/medium, 5 minor)

User cascade pivot decisions: defer Phase 14 → defer all joins → remove all watermarks (permanent) → remove event-time entirely (permanent) → session windows are simple incremental → mio is the only hot-path entry

Answered 5 design questions: (Q1) event_time_ms REMOVED from wire entirely [user override of recommend-keep]; (Q2) windowed ops switch to now_ms() server clock — Path X preserves 55-op catalogue; (Q3) session windows close on BOTH lazy-on-query AND flip-on-next-event-after-gap; (Q4) latest closed session retained per (entity, feature); (Q5) bv.union deferred with joins to v0.1+
Memory updates: NEW project_redis_shaped_no_event_time_ever.md (permanent commitment); MODIFIED project_stateful_architecture.md (Decision 2 PIT marked SUPERSEDED); UPDATED MEMORY.md index

Archived 5 phase dirs via git mv to `.planning/phases/_archived-*`: 14-streaming-correctness, 14.1-streaming-modifiability, 15-event-time-pit, 15-snapshot-io-off-main-thread, 14-per-stream-locks-dashmap-concurrency

Updated PROJECT.md (What This Is + Core Value + Requirements + Out of Scope + Constraints + Key Decisions); REQUIREMENTS.md (SDK-JOIN/JOIN-EXTRA/UNION-RECONCILE/SRV-APPLY-08/TEST-07/SDK-DEC-08 removed; many event_time refs swapped to now_ms(); SDK-SESSION-01 stub added); ROADMAP.md (Phase 12 retitled, 12.5/14/14.1/15 archived, 17 reworked, NEW 12.6 + 25 inserted); STATE.md (architectural pivot block + revised v0 critical path); CORRECTNESS-PATH.md (P0 list rewritten — Phase 14 bug class disappears with event-time itself)
Single doc-only commit 2d58d80 — 32 files changed, 231/129 inserts/deletes; no code changes

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Permanent: no event-time anywhere in Beava — ever
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: All future plans/discussions must respect this. Reviving requires explicit user override + new ADR.

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Permanent: no watermarks, no late-event correction, no PIT temporal store, no joins (event↔event/event↔table/table↔table)
mio data plane is the only hot-path entry; legacy axum scheduled for removal in Phase 12.6
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: All push/get/upsert/delete dispatches via apply_shard.rs::dispatch_*_sync; tokio sidecar only for admin endpoints

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Session windows replace event-time grouping for activity aggregation
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: New Phase 25 in ROADMAP (v0.1+); 5-7 plans; not v0 ship-blocker

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: event_time_ms REMOVED from wire entirely (push payload + WAL schema bump)
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: Phase 12.6 includes wire schema version bump + Python SDK update + tests + recipes; existing clients sending event_time_ms will get a register/push error post-12.6

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Path X: 14+ windowed ops switch to server-side now_ms() (preserves 55-op catalogue)
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: Phase 12.6 swap is mechanical — read now_ms() instead of event_time_ms in WindowedOp::update across all ops

key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.decision: Phase 12.6 scope expanded to include dead-code sweep + redundancy sweep + mio-only enforcement
key_locked_decisions_carried_forward.implication: 12-15 plans expected; ~3-4 weeks; biggest single v0 blocker

next_action_recommended: /gsd-discuss-phase 12.6 — gather scope-specific decisions for v0 surface reduction. Discuss should surface gray areas: (a) TestServerV18 harness shape — drop-in replacement vs new test pattern; (b) wire schema bump strategy — new version handle old+new for one release vs hard cutover; (c) dead-code threshold for 'sweep complete' (cargo-deadcode % target); (d) smoke-test fate per file (migrate vs delete vs replicate elsewhere); (e) REQUIREMENTS sweep depth (touch every event_time/join reference vs just the SDK-JOIN section); (f) docs touch scope (beava.dev guide pages, recipes — some require website work alongside); (g) phase-12-joins branch handling during merge — joins plumbing is now dead architecture; (h) plan ordering — kill axum first vs strip event-time first vs interleave
context_notes: User explicitly framed v0 polish as the most important thing. Phase 12.6 (surface reduction) + Phase 13 minimum (packaging + quickstart + readme + website final) is the polish path. Plan 12-10 push-and-get DEFERRED ("we probably dont need B for now"). phase11_smoke folds into 12.6's TestServerV18 migration. Don't auto-advance — let user open Phase 12.6 discuss fresh next session with full attention. The architectural commitment is FINAL — no more pivot-shaped questions next session, only scope-shaped questions about how to execute the locked direction. If next session surfaces a request to revisit any of the permanent architectural decisions (event-time, joins, watermarks, PIT), surface it as an explicit override request requiring a new ADR.
